current_role: foundational arithmetic and divisibility helper layer
rationale: This module exists to keep later theorem carriers readable; it does not present a standalone outward claim family.

current_role: prime-tour remainder-orbit semantics and period support
rationale: Its theorems support the public QR and digit surfaces, but the current atlas claims are better presented through the more targeted QR and digit modules.
The bridge recurrence infrastructure is essential, but the current public claim surfaces are clearer when carried by the signed and block-value specializations.
This module explains the subgroup picture used by the QR story, but it is support for the public claim rather than a separate atlas-sized theorem family.
This module contains the exact theorem family the atlas now cites for QR-generating stride classification and counting.

current_role: atlas-backed q-weighted series and body/correction algebra core
rationale: This is the canonical Lean carrier for the q-weighted geometric identity and the good-mode positivity boundary.

current_role: digit and remainder Euclidean equation plus digit periodicity
rationale: The digit-periodicity claim is compact, reusable, and best carried by this module directly.

current_role: general generator hierarchy above the QR-specific public surface
promotion_decision: leave as supporting infrastructure for now
rationale: The module is mathematically useful, but its current contents are broader than the repo's present public claim boundary.

promotion_decision: leave as supporting infrastructure for now
rationale: This remains research support rather than a single stable theorem family suitable for a claim ID.

current_role: signed plus/minus bridge recurrence package with sign cancellation
rationale: The signed recurrence and two-step cancellation law form a clear public theorem package.

current_role: bridge block-value geometricity and periodicity layer
rationale: This module supports exactly one crisp outward claim: block values along bridge strides form a geometric sequence with the expected period.

current_role: CRT order and composite period theorem carrier
rationale: The current classical-with-Lean-backing composite period claim lands naturally here.

current_role: valuation and base-factor preperiod theorem carrier
rationale: This is the exact Lean location for the local and global arithmetic beneath the repo's preperiod statement.

current_role: incoming-carry theorem surface and exact fixed-window lookahead certificate layer
promotion_decision: keep as public theorem surface with open-boundary support
rationale: This module now carries the exact fixed-window visibility theorem layer while still stopping short of the global open visibility claim.

current_role: finite carry normalization and traced transducer core
promotion_decision: keep as public support surface under the open carry boundary
rationale: The finite deterministic carry layer is exact and public, but it is still support beneath the open global factorization claim.

current_role: explicit finite carry and remainder comparison layer
promotion_decision: keep as public support surface under the open carry boundary
rationale: This module packages certified finite-word and finite-trace agreement without overstating the still-open global DFA statement.

current_role: same-core family packaging, endpoint labels, and coordinate-selection layer
promotion_decision: keep as public support surface tied to exact same-core laws
rationale: It turns the preperiod arithmetic into exact same-core shift theorems and selection criteria, but it does not yet close the minimal-lookahead frontier.

current_role: worked Lean acceptance examples and theorem smoke tests
promotion_decision: keep as public example surface, not atlas-backed
rationale: The file is valuable as a compact acceptance layer, but its purpose is validation and exposition rather than new outward claim transport.

current_role: companion overflow and decomposition library family
rationale: The GeometricStack family underpins some digit and overflow viewpoints, but it remains a companion library rather than a direct atlas-backed theorem surface.
